heading: Imposition and rate of tax
label: 5041 Imposition and rate of tax
notes: Codification, , , provided that, for single taxpayer rules relating to wine, see the cross reference under subsec. (c)(3) of this section.Pub. L. 116–260, div. EE, title I, § 110(b)134 Stat. 3049Dec. 27, 2020

Amendments2020—Subsec. (b)(1), (2). , substituted “16 percent” for “14 percent (16 percent in the case of wine removed after , and before ”.Pub. L. 116–260, § 106(e)(1)December 31, 2017January 1, 2021Subsec. (c). , struck out “for small domestic producers” after “Credit” in heading.Pub. L. 116–260, § 106(d)(1)(A)Subsec. (c)(1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Except as provided in paragraph (2), in the case of a person who produces not more than 250,000 wine gallons of wine during the calendar year, there shall be allowed as a credit against any tax imposed by this title (other than chapters 2, 21, and 22) of 90 cents per wine gallon on the 1st 100,000 wine gallons of wine (other than wine described in subsection (b)(4)) which are removed during such year for consumption or sale and which have been produced at qualified facilities in the United States. In the case of wine described in subsection (b)(6), the preceding sentence shall be applied by substituting ‘5.6 cents’ for ‘90 cents’.”Pub. L. 116–260, § 106(d)(1)(B)Subsec. (c)(1)(A). , inserted “but only if the importer is an electing importer under paragraph (6) and the wine gallons of wine have been assigned to the importer pursuant to such paragraph” after “into the United States during the calendar year” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 116–260, § 106(d)(2)(A)Subsec. (c)(2) to (5). , (D), redesignated pars. (3) to (6) as (2) to (5), respectively, and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text of par. (2) read as follows: “The credit allowable by paragraph (1) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by 1 percent for each 1,000 wine gallons of wine produced in excess of 150,000 wine gallons of wine during the calendar year.”Pub. L. 116–260, § 106(d)(1)(C)Subsec. (c)(6). , which directed only the redesignation of par. (9) as (6), was executed by redesignating par. (9) as (6) and transferring it so as to appear after par. (5), to reflect the probable intent of Congress. Former par. (6) redesignated (5).Pub. L. 116–260, § 106(d)(1)(E)Subsec. (c)(6)(A). , substituted “paragraph (1)” for “paragraph (8)”.Pub. children.heading: Imposition
children.label: a Imposition
children.text: There is hereby imposed on all wines (including imitation, substandard, or artificial wine, and compounds sold as wine) having not in excess of 24 percent of alcohol by volume, in bond in, produced in, or imported into, the United States, taxes at the rates shown in subsection (b), such taxes to be determined as of the time of removal for consumption or sale. All wines containing more than 24 percent of alcohol by volume shall be classed as distilled spirits and taxed accordingly. Subject to subsection (h), still wines shall include those wines containing not more than 0.392 gram of carbon dioxide per hundred milliliters of wine; except that the Secretary may by regulations prescribe such tolerances to this maximum limitation as may be reasonably necessary in good commercial practice.
On still wines containing not more than 16 percent of alcohol by volume, $1.07 per wine gallon;
On still wines containing more than 16 percent and not exceeding 21 percent of alcohol by volume, $1.57 per wine gallon;
On still wines containing more than 21 percent and not exceeding 24 percent of alcohol by volume, $3.15 per wine gallon;
On champagne and other sparkling wines, $3.40 per wine gallon;
On artificially carbonated wines, $3.30 per wine gallon; and
On hard cider, 22.6 cents per wine gallon.

children.label: i
children.text: $1 per wine gallon on the first 30,000 wine gallons of wine, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: 90 cents per wine gallon on the first 100,000 wine gallons of wine to which clause (i) does not apply, plus

children.label: iii
children.text: 53.5 cents per wine gallon on the first 620,000 wine gallons of wine to which clauses (i) and (ii) do not apply,

children.heading: Adjustment of credit for hard cider
children.label: B Adjustment of credit for hard cider
i
children.text: in clause (i) of such subparagraph, by substituting “6.2 cents” for “$1”,

children.label: ii
children.text: in clause (ii) of such subparagraph, by substituting “5.6 cents” for “90 cents”, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in clause (iii) of such subparagraph, by substituting “3.3 cents” for “53.5 cents”.

children.heading: Time for determining and allowing credit
children.label: 2 Time for determining and allowing credit
A
children.text: shall be determined at the same time the tax is determined under subsection (a) of this section, and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be allowable at the time any tax described in paragraph (1) is payable as if the credit allowable by this subsection constituted a reduction in the rate of such tax.

children.heading: Controlled groups1See Codification note below.1
children.label: 3 Controlled groups1See Codification note below.1
children.text: Rules similar to rules of section 5051(a)(5) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Denial of deduction
children.label: 4 Denial of deduction
children.text: Any deduction under subtitle A with respect to any tax against which a credit is allowed under this subsection shall only be for the amount of such tax as reduced by such credit.

children.label: A
children.text: wine produced by any person would be eligible for any credit under paragraph (1) if removed by such person during the calendar year,

children.label: B
children.text: wine produced by such person is removed during such calendar year by any other person (hereafter in this paragraph referred to as the “transferee”) to whom such wine was transferred in bond and who is liable for the tax imposed by this section with respect to such wine, and

children.label: C
children.text: such producer holds title to such wine at the time of its removal and provides to the transferee such information as is necessary to properly determine the transferee’s credit under this paragraph,

children.heading: Allowance of credit for foreign manufacturers and importers
children.label: 6 Allowance of credit for foreign manufacturers and importers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any wine gallons of wine which have been produced outside of the United States and imported into the United States, the credit allowable under paragraph (1) (referred to in this paragraph as the “tax credit”) may be assigned by the person who produced such wine (referred to in this paragraph as the “foreign producer”), provided that such person makes an election described in subparagraph (B)(ii), to any electing importer of such wine gallons pursuant to the requirements established by the Secretary under subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: to any importer does not exceed the number of wine gallons of wine produced by such foreign producer during the calendar year which were imported into the United States by such importer, and

children.label: II
children.text: to all importers does not exceed the 750,000 wine gallons of wine to which the tax credit applies,

children.label: ii
children.text: procedures that allow the election of a foreign producer to assign and an importer to receive the tax credit provided under this paragraph,

children.label: iii
children.text: requirements that the foreign producer provide any information as the Secretary determines necessary and appropriate for purposes of carrying out this paragraph, and

children.label: iv
children.text: procedures that allow for revocation of eligibility of the foreign producer and the importer for the tax credit provided under this paragraph in the case of any erroneous or fraudulent information provided under clause (iii) which the Secretary deems to be material to qualifying for such credit.

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: C Controlled group
children.text: For purposes of this section, any importer making an election described in subparagraph (B)(ii) shall be deemed to be a member of the controlled group of the foreign producer, as described under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Refunds in lieu of tax credits for foreign production removed afterDecember 31, 2022
children.label: 7 Refunds in lieu of tax credits for foreign production removed afterDecember 31, 2022
i
children.text: paragraph (1) shall not apply, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount determined under subparagraph (B) shall be allowed as a refund, determined for periods not less frequently than quarterly, to the importer in the same manner as if such amount were an overpayment of tax imposed by this section.

children.label: I
children.text: the amount of tax imposed under this section on wine gallons of wine referred to in subparagraph (A) which were removed during such period, over

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of tax which would have been imposed under this section (including any allowable credits) on such gallons of wine if this section were applied without regard to this paragraph, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of interest which would be allowed and paid on an overpayment of tax at the overpayment rate established under section 6621(a)(1) (without regard to the second sentence thereof) were such rate applied to the excess (if any) determined under clause (i) for the number of days in the filing period for which the refund under this paragraph is being determined.

children.heading: Application of rules related to elections and assignments
children.label: C Application of rules related to elections and assignments
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(ii) shall apply only if the importer is an electing importer under paragraph (6) and the wine gallons of wine have been assigned to the importer pursuant to such paragraph.

children.heading: Rules for refunds within 90 days
children.label: D Rules for refunds within 90 days
children.text: For purposes of refunds allowed under this paragraph, section 6611(e) shall be applied by substituting “90 days” for “45 days” each place it appears.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 8 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations to ensure proper calculation of the credit provided in this subsection.

children.heading: Wine gallon
children.label: d Wine gallon
children.text: For the purpose of this chapter, the term “wine gallon” means a United States gallon of liquid measure equivalent to the volume of 231 cubic inches. On lesser quantities the tax shall be paid proportionately (fractions of less than one-tenth gallon being converted to the nearest one-tenth gallon, and five-hundredths gallon being converted to the next full one-tenth gallon).

children.heading: Tolerances
children.label: e Tolerances
children.text: Where the Secretary finds that the revenue will not be endangered thereby, he may by regulation prescribe tolerances (but not greater than ½ of 1 percent) for bottles and other containers, and, if such tolerances are prescribed, no assessment shall be made and no tax shall be collected for any excess in any case where the contents of a bottle or other container are within the limit of the applicable tolerance prescribed.

children.heading: Illegally produced wine
children.label: f Illegally produced wine
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), any wine produced in the United States at any place other than the bonded premises provided for in this chapter shall (except as provided in section 5042 in the case of tax-free production) be subject to tax at the rate prescribed in subsection (b) at the time of production and whether or not removed for consumption or sale.

containing not more than 0.64 gram of carbon dioxide per hundred milliliters of wine, except that the Secretary may by regulations prescribe such tolerances to this limitation as may be reasonably necessary in good commercial practice,

children.label: A
children.text: from apples or pears, or

children.label: i
children.text: apple juice concentrate or pear juice concentrate, and

children.label: ii
children.text: water,
which contains no fruit product or fruit flavoring other than apple or pear, and
which contains at least one-half of 1 percent and less than 8.5 percent alcohol by volume.

children.heading: Mead and low alcohol by volume wine
children.label: h Mead and low alcohol by volume wine

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsections (a) and (b)(1), mead and low alcohol by volume wine shall be deemed to be still wines containing not more than 16 percent of alcohol by volume.

children.label: i
children.text: containing not more than 0.64 gram of carbon dioxide per hundred milliliters of wine, except that the Secretary may by regulations prescribe such tolerances to this limitation as may be reasonably necessary in good commercial practice,

children.label: ii
children.text: which is derived solely from honey and water,

children.label: iii
children.text: which contains no fruit product or fruit flavoring, and

children.label: iv
children.text: which contains less than 8.5 percent alcohol by volume.

children.heading: Low alcohol by volume wine
children.label: B Low alcohol by volume wine
i
children.text: containing not more than 0.64 gram of carbon dioxide per hundred milliliters of wine, except that the Secretary may by regulations prescribe such tolerances to this limitation as may be reasonably necessary in good commercial practice,

children.label: I
children.text: primarily from grapes, or

children.label: II
children.text: from grape juice concentrate and water,

children.label: iii
children.text: which contains no fruit product or fruit flavoring other than grape, and

children.label: iv
children.text: which contains less than 8.5 percent alcohol by volume.
